---
title: "Talks & Events"
---

## Talks

::: {.pub-entry}
**Selecting the Number of Clusters in a Clustering Algorithm**
[*Journées de la Statistique* (SFDS), June 2025]{.pub-venue}
Oral presentation introducing a data-driven model selection criterion for choosing the number of classes in the Spectral Bridges clustering algorithm. The method leverages the scale-invariant affinity structure of the algorithm to define a stability-based score, avoiding cross-validation and working directly from the spectral decomposition.
:::

::: {.pub-entry}
**Uniform High-Probability Bands for Survival Functions**
[*StatMathAppli*, September 2025 — Poster]{.pub-venue}
Poster presenting non-asymptotic uniform confidence bands for survival estimators derived under a joint modelling framework. The bands hold with high probability uniformly over time, providing rigorous inferential guarantees for censored survival data beyond classical pointwise confidence intervals.
:::

---

## Attended Events

| Date | Event |
|---|---|
| June 2025 | Working group on Causality — MIA Paris-Saclay |
| July 2025 | Working group on Topological Data Analysis — LaMME |
| October 2025 | Winter School on Causality and Explainable AI |
| March 2026 | Workshop on Uncertainty Quantification and Causality |
